prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response:
Arrr! Listen well, ye scurvy landlubber, and I’ll tell ye why the sky be blue as a mermaid’s eye!

’Tis the sun’s own light—white as a ghost’s shroud—that sails through the heavens. But when it strikes the air above us, full o’ tiny, invisible bits o’ sea-spray and breath o’ the world, the light doth scatter like cannon shot in a squall! And lo—the blue bits, bein’ the spryest and lightest o’ the lot, bounce about the most, fillin’ the whole dome o’ the sky with their sapphire glare.

So when ye squint up at noonday, ye ain’t seein’ the sun’s true face—nay! Ye be seein’ the blue light kicked ‘round by the very air itself, like a deckhand tossed by a rogue wave! Red and yellow? They’re too stout and heavy—they sail straight on, savin’ their fire for sunset, when the sky burns like Davy Jones’ own forge!

So there ye have it, matey—blue sky’s naught but sunlight dancin’ with the wind! Now fetch me a grog, or I’ll keelhaul ye for askin’ such a clever question!
